evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses correct v1 endpoint https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete with X-Goog-Api-Key and X-Goog-FieldMask headers, JSON body with input and sessionToken — all consistent with Places API (New). Internally consistent across modules.
evidence.executability: Code blocks parse; fastapi==0.135.1 is a suspicious/likely-nonexistent pinned version (latest stable is in 0.11x range as of 2024), which could cause pip install to fail. Otherwise imports defined, no sandbox blockers.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: API key acquisition with Cloud Console steps, pinned pip/npm installs in bash blocks, complete .env.example, backend code (config, schemas, client, main), frontend code (hook, component, App, index), and test commands (curl + pytest + npm test). Env vars listed match referenced.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Numbered citation references [1][2][7] etc. appear throughout but no actual URLs are provided anywhere in the playbook — no canonical developers.google.com/maps URLs cited.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; long-tail handling applied. Internal consistency is strong and version-claimed (Places API New v1), supporting a 4 on correctness. Citations are bare numeric markers with no URLs — fails canonical anchor test, score 1. Pinned fastapi==0.135.1 is dubious but not catastrophic; capped executability at 4.
